script.narrator: Welcome, friend. Today we're going to meet something that sounds fancy — 'Artificial Intelligence' — but it's really just a super-smart puppy that learns from examples. You don't need a computer degree. You need the same patience you'd use training a dear old dog.

onScreen.headline: AI Is Like a Super-Smart Puppy
onScreen.visual: gentle animation of puppy watching owner bake

interaction.prompt: Say 'Hello, puppy' or tap the button if you'd like me to continue.

script.narrator: Imagine you teach your puppy to fetch slippers. You don't explain physics — you show slippers, say 'fetch,' and give a treat when he gets it right. After many tries, he spots the pattern: soft thing by the bed equals fetch time. Machine learning works the same way. We show the computer thousands of examples — pictures of cats, voices saying 'hello' — and it learns patterns. It is not magic. It is practice, like your puppy.

onScreen.headline: Learning = Seeing Patterns Over and Over
onScreen.visual: split screen: puppy treats / computer labeled photos
onScreen.callout: The computer does NOT understand like a person. It recognizes patterns.

interaction.question: What does the puppy — and the computer — need most to learn?

options.label: Lots of good examples and gentle correction

interaction.feedbackCorrect: Exactly. Examples and patience — just like with any good pupil.
interaction.feedbackWrong: Not quite. Remember: slippers, treats, repeat. Examples teach the pattern.

script.narrator: Now, puppies make mistakes. They might bring you a shoe instead of a slipper. AI makes mistakes too — sometimes it thinks a fake video is real, or treats everyone unfairly because it only saw one kind of face growing up. That is called bias. Your job — your wisdom — is to be the loving owner who says, 'No, not that one,' and guides it. AI will not replace you. It needs your judgment, your kindness, and your years of common sense.

onScreen.headline: You Are the Teacher, Not the Student
onScreen.visual: elderly person gently correcting puppy with smile
onScreen.safetyTip: If a phone call sounds like your grandchild asking for money — pause. Scammers use AI voices. Call back on a number you know.

interaction.prompt: Tap two things YOU will remember today:
items: AI learns patterns from examples — like a puppy
AI can be wrong or biased — I stay in charge
I will pause before trusting urgent voice or video messages

script.narrator: Wonderful. You've just learned more about AI than most headlines will tell you. Next time, we'll practice spotting trick pictures together. For now, give yourself a pat — you've trained your super-smart puppy.
